goal_statement: Enhance the AI agent skills framework to support hierarchical EDPS methodology with boundary concepts, enabling multi-level process decomposition that transforms single-level collaboration diagrams into structured, navigable process hierarchies with proper participant stereotyping and organizational model integration.

success_criteria.criteria: Generate hierarchical collaboration diagrams with Mermaid box syntax boundaries at 5+ levels of decomposition
Classify all participants using stereotypes (actor, boundary, control, entity) with correct decomposition rule enforcement
Maintain 100% backward compatibility with Project 1 diagram outputs
Automatically create and organize sub-folder structures for process hierarchies
Enforce EDPS methodology compliance across all generated artifacts
Update OrgModel to reflect hierarchical EDPS methodology with boundary concepts
Convert 100% of Project 1 diagrams to hierarchical format via migration tools

kpis.kpi: Hierarchy depth support — 5+ levels tested and validated
Boundary accuracy — 100% correct boundary identification in test cases
Diagram generation performance — ≤30 seconds for hierarchical diagram generation
Migration success rate — 100% of Project 1 diagrams convertible
User adoption rate — >90% of developers using hierarchical modeling
EDPS compliance score — 100% validation pass rate
Backward compatibility — 0 breaking changes to existing skill interfaces

constraints.constraint: Must use Mermaid box syntax for boundaries — no native boundary element
Must maintain seamless VS Code and GitHub Copilot integration
Must preserve markdown-based workflow without external tooling dependencies
Cannot introduce breaking changes to existing Project 1 skill interfaces

assumptions.assumption: Mermaid box syntax will remain stable and supported in VS Code Mermaid preview
All Project 1 skills are fully functional as prerequisites
Participant stereotype syntax is supported by Mermaid rendering
Existing OrgModel structure is accessible and modifiable
Teams have foundational EDPS methodology understanding from Project 1

open_questions.question: What is the maximum practical hierarchy depth before navigation becomes unwieldy?
Should boundary detection be fully automatic or require user confirmation for ambiguous cases?
How should circular dependencies between process hierarchies be handled?
What Mermaid rendering limitations exist for deeply nested box syntax?
Should new OrgModel process areas be created or only existing ones enhanced?
What is the migration priority order for Project 1 artifacts?
